channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Canada's Trudeau to shuffle his Cabinet Friday amid resignation calls and rising discontent
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Embattled Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau will shuffle his Cabinet Friday, two senior officials familiar with the matter said Thursday. The officials confirmed the shuffle and spoke on condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak publicly on the matter.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor charged in a bribery conspiracy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The New York City mayor's former chief adviser, her son and two real estate investors were charged Thursday in a bribery conspiracy, charges that come as the mayor himself faces a separate corruption case and a series of criminal investigations roils City Hall.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government, or federal agencies will shutter.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Spending bill contains relief for border farmers affected by lack of water
item.description: A spending bill that Congress is debating to continue funding the federal government past Friday also contains funding relief for farmers on the South Texas border who have been affected by a lack of water payments from Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Weird laws in Colorado, including one against throwing snowballs
item.description: Every state has its own strange laws, and Colorado is no exception. In the state, laws against throwing snowballs and buying cars on Sundays are both on the books.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas train derails after hitting tractor-trailer, barreling into city building: video
item.description: One person is dead and four others are injured after a train hit a tractor-trailer on the railroad tracks in Pecos, Texas, causing it to derail and strike the city's Chamber of Commerce building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee dog to be reunited with family after agencies team up to rescue him from ravine: video
item.description: A Tennessee dog by the name of Apollo will be back home for the holidays after he was found by local and state agencies injured and stuck in a ravine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California man who messaged Wisconsin shooter, planned own attack ordered to surrender guns
item.description: The shooter who killed a student and teacher at a religious school in Wisconsin brought two guns to the school and was in contact with a man in California who authorities say was planning to attack a government building, according to authorities and court documents that became public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Commerce Department's efforts to curb China's and Russia's access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons, according to a report published Wednesday by the Senate's Perman
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rudy Giuliani ordered to appear at contempt hearing in January over failure to give up assets
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered Rudy Giuliani to appear in court in early January to face claims that he should be held in contempt for failing to turn over assets including a New York apartment lease and a signed Joe DiMaggio shirt to two Georgia election poll workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 pilots die in fiery small plane crash into vacant building near Honolulu airport
item.description: Shortly after taking off from Honolulu's Daniel K. Inouye International Airport, two pilots were killed when their small plane on a training flight crashed into a vacant building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Man accused of running secret Chinese gov police station in NYC makes plea: US Attorney
item.description: One of two New York City residents accused of operating a secret Chinese police station in Chinatown pleaded guilty in federal court to conspiring to act as an agent of China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chicago White Sox stadium getting a new name for the 2025 season
item.description: The home stadium of the Chicago White Sox won't be called Guaranteed Rate Field come the start of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: NCAA president says there are 'less than 10' transgender athletes in college sports
item.description: NCAA President Charlie Baker told a Senate panel that there are fewer than 10 transgender athletes he is aware of who currently compete in college sports, pouring cold water on an issue Republicans have said is a nationwide problem and one that is increasingly fraught territory for Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida pursues charge against suspect in Trump assassination attempt for a crash after his arrest
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida authorities announced Wednesday that they're pursuing a criminal case against a man accused of trying to assassinate President-elect Donald Trump for a car crash that occurred after law enforcement stopped traffic to catch him.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New charges target ex-Miami congressman for lobbying on behalf of sanctioned Venezuelan tycoon
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal grand jury in Washington has returned an indictment against former Congressman David Rivera for failing to register as a foreign agent of a sanctioned Venezuelan media tycoon on whose behalf the Miami Republican allegedly lobbied the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former New Orleans priest, 93, is sentenced to life in prison for raping boy decades ago
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A judge sentenced a 93-year-old former Catholic priest Wednesday to spend the rest of his life behind bars for raping a teenage boy decades ago.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alleged would-be Trump assassin faces new charge in accident that nearly killed child: Florida authorities
item.description: The state of Florida has charged Ryan Wesley Routh with attempted felony murder in connection with a traffic accident that occurred when he fled police after an alleged assassination attempt on former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: This Colorado restaurant only has 2 items on the menu
item.description: A Michelin-starred chef co-opened a restaurant in Denver with only two items on the menu: a burger and fries.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service warns of 'smishing,' other holiday scams: What to know
item.description: “While the holiday season offers merriment, joy, and togetherness, fraudsters and their scams can dim the season,” the Postal Service’s federal law enforcement arm warned.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, ABC 4

item.title: Hochul vows to haul Luigi Mangione back to New York to face terror charges in UnitedHealthcare CEO's slaying
item.description: New York Gov. Kathy Hochul demanded UnitedHealthcare CEO suspected killer Luigi Mangione be brought back from Pennsylvania to face new terrorism charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaetz defends ‘embarrassing’ past behavior, fires shot at Ethics report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) is defending what he says is "embarrassing, though not criminal" past behavior and taking a shot at the House Ethics Committee, following reports that the panel voted to release its findings about him.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa native named starting QB for Atlanta Falcons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The Atlanta Falcons are turning to rookie Michael Penix Jr. as their starting quarterback and benching veteran Kirk Cousins as they fight for their first playoff berth since 2017. Coach Raheem Morris announced the decision in a statement Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM AP Sports Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Over 500,000 winter tires recalled due to poor traction in snow
item.description: A tire company that sells winter tires has issued a recall for more than 540,000 of its snow tires, which do not comply with North American standards due to lack of proper traction on snow.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Indiana law shrouds executions in secrecy, prompting new pushes for public oversight
item.description: From public hangings in the town square to lethal injections witnessed by journalists, executions historically have mostly been carried out with at least some public scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to claim a 2nd payment from the Equifax data breach settlement
item.description: Equifax has started alerting people of more money waiting for them from a massive fund the credit reporting agency paid to settle a data breach.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, was charged in New York with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors announced Tuesday. Mangione, 26, is being held without bail in Pennsylvania, where he was arrested at a McDonald’s on Dec.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KTLA 5

item.title: Tax brackets and more are changing in 2024, 2025: What to know
item.description: We're more than 100 days out from Tax Day, but there are some impending updates you may want to prepare for.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Rear seat belt reminders to be required in 2027
item.description: NHTSA estimated the new rule will prevent more than 500 injuries and save about 50 lives each year if and when it's fully implemented.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: NBA unveils new All-Star format featuring four-team tournament
item.description: The NBA 's All-Star Game is going to be an All-Star tournament this season, with the league announcing on Tuesday that it has finalized plans to use a different format for the upcoming midseason showcase in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about the victims in Wisconsin Christian school shooting
item.description: A 14-year-old girl and a substitute teacher were killed in Monday's shooting. Another teacher and five students were injured.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5
